name: aws-cdk-aws-logs-destinations

metadata.name: aws-cdk.aws-logs-destinations
metadata.summary: Log Destinations for AWS CloudWatch Logs

documentation:
# CDK Construct Libray for AWS XXX

<!--BEGIN STABILITY BANNER-->---


![cdk-constructs: Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/cdk--constructs-stable-success.svg?style=for-the-badge)

---
<!--END STABILITY BANNER-->

This library contains destinations for AWS CloudWatch Logs SubscriptionFilters. You
can send log data to Kinesis Streams or Lambda Functions.

See the documentation of the `logs` module for more information.

modules.name: aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations
documentation:
# CDK Construct Libray for AWS XXX

<!--BEGIN STABILITY BANNER-->---


![cdk-constructs: Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/cdk--constructs-stable-success.svg?style=for-the-badge)

---
<!--END STABILITY BANNER-->

This library contains destinations for AWS CloudWatch Logs SubscriptionFilters. You
can send log data to Kinesis Streams or Lambda Functions.

See the documentation of the `logs` module for more information.

classes.name: aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations.KinesisDestination

methods.name: aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations.KinesisDestination.__init__
documentation:
:param stream: The Kinesis stream to use as destination.
:param role: The role to assume to write log events to the destination. Default: - A new Role is created

methods.name: aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations.KinesisDestination.bind
documentation:
Return the properties required to send subscription events to this destination.

If necessary, the destination can use the properties of the SubscriptionFilter
object itself to configure its permissions to allow the subscription to write
to it.

The destination may reconfigure its own permissions in response to this
function call.

:param scope: -
:param _source_log_group: -

Use a Kinesis stream as the destination for a log subscription.

Example:

```
# The code below shows an example of how to instantiate this type.
# The values are placeholders you should change.
import aws_cdk.aws_iam as iam
import aws_cdk.aws_kinesis as kinesis
import aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations as logs_destinations

# role: iam.Role
# stream: kinesis.Stream

kinesis_destination = logs_destinations.KinesisDestination(stream,
    role=role
)
```

classes.name: aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations.LambdaDestination

methods.name: aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations.LambdaDestination.__init__
documentation:
LambdaDestinationOptions.

:param fn: -
:param add_permissions: Whether or not to add Lambda Permissions. Default: true

methods.name: aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations.LambdaDestination.bind
documentation:
Return the properties required to send subscription events to this destination.

If necessary, the destination can use the properties of the SubscriptionFilter
object itself to configure its permissions to allow the subscription to write
to it.

The destination may reconfigure its own permissions in response to this
function call.

:param scope: -
:param log_group: -

Use a Lambda Function as the destination for a log subscription.

Example:

```
import aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations as destinations
# fn: lambda.Function
# log_group: logs.LogGroup


logs.SubscriptionFilter(self, "Subscription",
    log_group=log_group,
    destination=destinations.LambdaDestination(fn),
    filter_pattern=logs.FilterPattern.all_terms("ERROR", "MainThread")
)
```

methods.name: aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations.LambdaDestinationOptions.__init__
documentation:

```{eval-rst}

:exampleMetadata: fixture=_generated

```

```
lambda_destination_options = logs_destinations.LambdaDestinationOptions(
    add_permissions=False
)
```

methods.name: aws_cdk.aws_logs_destinations.LambdaDestinationOptions.add_permissions
documentation:
Whether or not to add Lambda Permissions.

:default: true
